title: 4x8 Chicken Coop
summary: A raised-floor 4x8 chicken coop with stick-framed walls, a nest box, and a shed roof with angled trusses, sided in T1-11 panels.
description:
A coop this size needs to solve three problems at once: keeping the flock warm and dry, keeping predators out, and staying easy enough to clean that you'll actually do it. The raised 2x6 floor frame handles drainage and pest access from below, T1-11 siding gives a weathertight and paintable exterior in one material, and a wire-mesh floor opening plus mesh-covered door section handle ventilation and predator exclusion without extra hardware.

The roof trusses are cut at a **40° angle on both ends, in the same direction** — a shed-style single-slope roof rather than a peaked one, which sheds water off the back and is a simpler truss to cut correctly than a ridge roof. Half the trusses get a **bird's-mouth notch** where they land on the wall top plate; the other half don't need one, so keep the two groups sorted before you start standing them.

The nest box is referenced in the source as a separate linked plan and isn't detailed here — budget separate time and material for it, or substitute a simpler shelf-and-divider box of your own design.

tools.note: Attaches wire mesh and roofing paper.

materials.name: Construction lumber, 2x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Wall framing, rafters, roof deck framing, and door framing.

materials.name: Deck screws and galvanized nails, assorted
Wire mesh
materials.note: Predator-proofs the floor opening and door screens.

cutList.note: 40-degree angle cuts on both ends, same direction. 6 of the 10 need a bird's-mouth notch where they meet the wall top plate.
Trim to fit the final roof outline.
Cut door and mesh-vent openings as you go.
Cut to size for the door and mesh-vent frames.

steps.title: Build the raised floor frame
steps.body: Build the floor frame from the 2x6 perimeter and cross joists per the cut list, squaring it up on a level site.
materials: Deck screws and galvanized nails, assorted

steps.title: Frame and stand the walls
steps.body: Frame each wall section separately from the 2x4 studs and plates, then stand and fasten all four wall frames to the floor frame.
materials: Deck screws and galvanized nails, assorted

steps.title: Build and install the roof trusses
steps.body: Cut and assemble the roof trusses with 40-degree angle cuts, notching the bird's-mouth into the half of the set that will sit on the wall top plate. Install the trusses onto the wall frames.
materials: Deck screws and galvanized nails, assorted

steps.title: Build the nest box and side the walls
steps.body: Build and install a nest box, then side the walls with T1-11 panels, cutting door and mesh-vent openings as you go.

steps.title: Hang the doors
steps.body: Frame and hang the door or doors, covering any wire-mesh vent openings with mesh stapled firmly in place.

steps.title: Install the roof deck and predator-proof the floor
steps.body: Install the roof deck plywood, then roofing paper and shingles. Staple wire mesh across the floor-frame opening for predator protection from below.
